original_hypothesis.title: MagicSpend Owner Can Drain Contract Including User-Allocated WithdrawableETH
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The ownerWithdraw function allows the MagicSpend owner to withdraw any amount of any asset to any address without regard for _withdrawableETH balances owed to users. If the owner withdraws ETH that is accounted for in _withdrawableETH, subsequent postOp calls or withdrawGasExcess calls will fail due to insufficient balance. Users who have pending _withdrawableETH credits (between validatePaymasterUserOp and postOp, or waiting to call withdrawGasExcess) lose their funds. There is no accounting check in ownerWithdraw to preserve user-allocated balances.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This creates a trust assumption where the MagicSpend owner must never withdraw more than the unallocated balance. The contract provides no mechanism to determine the unallocated balance or to prevent withdrawal of allocated funds.

proof_of_signal.pattern: ownerWithdraw calls _withdraw without checking against sum of _withdrawableETH balances

preconditions: MagicSpend owner calls ownerWithdraw for amount exceeding unallocated balance

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: ownerWithdraw at line 195 has no guard against draining user-allocated funds

operational_questions: Is the MagicSpend owner a multisig with adequate security?
Is there off-chain monitoring for owner withdrawals vs allocated balances?
What is the typical _withdrawableETH balance at any given time?
validation_requirements: Call ownerWithdraw for full balance while _withdrawableETH entries exist
Verify postOp/withdrawGasExcess failure after owner drain

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Compromise or collude with MagicSpend owner
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Time ownerWithdraw during active paymaster usage (pending _withdrawableETH)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Withdraw entire contract balance via ownerWithdraw
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Users' postOp and withdrawGasExcess calls fail

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: MagicSpend funds drained including user allocations
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Owner must re-deposit; user trust damaged
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: postOp, withdrawGasExcess for all affected users

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Contract balance dropping below sum of _withdrawableETH
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Owner withdrawals timed during high paymaster usage

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
